title: Do not default to three-column pricing tables with middle tier highlighted
summary: AI-generated pricing sections default to three identical columns with the middle plan highlighted as 'Popular' regardless of the product's actual pricing strategy.
instruction: Choose a pricing layout that reflects the product's actual pricing logic: tier differences, feature groupings, and the plan most users should choose. The three-column reflex with a highlighted middle tier is a template, not a pricing strategy.
rationale: The three-column pricing card with a 'Most Popular' middle badge is the most recognizable AI-generation template in SaaS marketing. It makes every pricing page look like every other pricing page, regardless of whether the product has three meaningful tiers or a different optimal plan structure.

violations: A pricing page with three equal-width columns, middle column highlighted with a 'Most Popular' badge, identical layout regardless of actual pricing differences.
A product with two pricing tiers and a 'Contact Us' enterprise option -- forced into three columns with the enterprise column having no price.
controls: A pricing page with two columns for the actual plan tiers plus a separate 'Enterprise' callout section.
A single-plan pricing page with a simple CTA, no column layout needed.
borderline: A product with three genuinely distinct pricing tiers where the three-column layout accurately represents the offering.

exceptions.description: Products with three genuinely distinct tiers where the three-column layout accurately reflects real pricing differences. A/B tested layouts where three columns outperformed alternatives.
examples: Legitimate three-tier SaaS with meaningful feature differences
Documented A/B test showing three-column preference

detector.signalDescription: Detect three equal-width card columns in a section with pricing-adjacent labels (price, month, plan, tier). Flag when middle column has a distinct visual treatment (highlighted border, badge).
limitations: Cannot distinguish genuine three-tier pricing from template reflex without understanding the product's actual pricing model.
Some products have legitimate three-tier offerings that match the template.

scenarios: A product with three genuinely differentiated pricing tiers.
An A/B tested layout where three columns and highlighted middle outperformed alternatives.
mitigations: Check for meaningful feature differentiation between columns beyond badge color. Accept documented A/B results.

fixturePlan.description: A pricing page with three equal columns, middle 'Most Popular' highlighted, identical card structure.
A pricing page with two plan columns plus an enterprise section below.
A product with three genuinely distinct plans documented with feature details.
